stem:
The table summarizes 180 patrons of a public library by genre preference and library card status.

Has cardNo cardTotal
Prefers fiction9636132
Prefers nonfiction301848
Total12654180

If a patron is selected at random, what is the probability that the selected patron prefers fiction?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. The probability is the number of fiction-preferring patrons divided by the total: 132/180.

Choice B is incorrect; this is the probability of having a card. Choice C is incorrect; this is the probability of not having a card. Choice D is incorrect; this is the probability of preferring nonfiction.
